component.inlineNotice: Unsaved changes stay local until you save.

fields.title: Text input
fields.conditionalVisibleText: Visible when advanced controls are enabled
fields.opsSecretHelp: Private credential used only on the server side.

charts.barDescription: Grouped and stacked bars with grid, axis, tooltip, and legend support.
charts.lineDescription: Multiple line series with shadcn chart tooltip and legend.
charts.areaDescription: Stackable area series for trend previews.
charts.pieDescription: Segment charts driven by dataKey and nameKey.
charts.donutDescription: Pie chart variant with configurable inner and outer radius.
charts.radialDescription: Radial bar chart for proportional status summaries.

tables.inlineDescription: Inline table with global search, pagination, sortable columns, badge cells, boolean cells, and a default sort.
tables.cardDescription: Full-card table with search, sorting, pagination, badges, and boolean cells.
tables.selectableDescription: Table selection with visible-page select all, shift-click range selection, and selected-row bulk action payloads.
tables.selectedRowsPreview: Selected row payload from the latest bulk action.
tables.selectedRowIdsPreview: Stable selected row IDs from the latest bulk action.
tables.filtersDescription: Column/operator/value filters for text, number, option, multi-option, boolean, and source-backed options.
tables.rowStateDescription: Click a row to route into a detail state and write the clicked row into runtime data.

simple.label: Simple settings drawer
simple.description: A small drawer with standard form controls.

createEntity.label: Create entity
createEntity.description: A realistic create flow with nested advanced settings.

advanced.label: Advanced mapping
advanced.description: Nested drawer content can include the same controls as the parent drawer.

editRule.label: Edit rule
editRule.description: Repeater rows can expose focused edit drawers for details that should not clutter the list.

preview.label: Preview drawer
preview.description: Drawer content can mix display elements and backend actions.
preview.text: This drawer combines static copy, structured preview data, and an action button.

previewNested.label: Bottom nested drawer level two
previewNested.description: Bottom drawers can stack upward while the preview drawer remains visible behind them.

previewNestedSecond.label: Bottom nested drawer level three
previewNestedSecond.description: The same nested drawer contract works from the bottom edge.
previewNestedSecond.text: The preview drawer stays mounted underneath this bottom drawer stack.

deep.label: Nested drawer level one
deep.description: Open nested drawers without leaving the parent drawer state.

deepSecond.label: Nested drawer level two
deepSecond.description: The parent drawer remains mounted while the child is open.

deepThird.label: Nested drawer level three
deepThird.description: Deep drawer content can still edit module settings.

leftDeep.label: Left nested drawer level one
leftDeep.description: Left drawers keep the previous level visible behind the active drawer.

leftDeepSecond.label: Left nested drawer level two
leftDeepSecond.description: The parent left drawer stays mounted while a deeper drawer opens.

leftDeepThird.label: Left nested drawer level three
leftDeepThird.description: Deep left drawer content can use the same module controls.
leftDeepThird.text: Left-side nested drawers reveal an even stack behind the active level.

topDeep.label: Top nested drawer level one
topDeep.description: Top drawers stack downward while preserving the parent drawer state.

topDeepSecond.label: Top nested drawer level two
topDeepSecond.description: The same drawer component supports vertical nesting from the top edge.

topDeepThird.label: Top nested drawer level three
topDeepThird.description: Deep top drawer content stays editable inside the active level.
topDeepThird.text: Top-side nested drawers reveal an even stack above the app content.

stress.label: Mini UI lab drawer
stress.description: A full renderer stress drawer containing every non-drawer UI Lab surface.

segments.label: Workflow segments
segments.description: Settings-backed entries with ordering, generated drawer sections, and related rules.
segments.deleteDescription: Selected segment rows will be removed from local module settings.

activity.label: Activity events
activity.description: Manual source-backed entries that send search, filters, sorting, and pagination to PHP.

contacts.label: Table-inferred contacts
contacts.description: Custom table entries whose list columns, filters, and drawer fields come from tables.php column metadata.
contacts.deleteDescription: Selected contact rows will be sent to the table-backed delete action.

miniApp.welcome: Launch setup starts with a short configuration flow.
miniApp.configure: Configure a few settings, then move to review.
miniApp.review: Review mode uses the same saved settings with a different view.
miniApp.complete: The mini app reached a final state without leaving the tab.

miniApps.contentStart: Compose a campaign brief from module settings, source-backed options, date controls, code, and a backend action.
miniApps.contentReview: The generated result below came from the PHP action and was written back into the renderer runtime data.
miniApps.operationsStart: Configure an operational audit, review the payload, then run a PHP action that returns rows and statuses.
miniApps.operationsReview: Review the structured payload before it is sent to the backend action.

help.sectionText: Examples where help adds context that the visible label cannot carry alone.
help.apiNamespaceText: Use a stable lowercase namespace. Changing it later can break existing integrations.
help.batchSizeText: Large batches can time out on slow hosts. Start low and increase after a successful dry run.
help.fallbackRoleText: Used only when a visitor does not match a more specific role rule.
help.dryRunText: Dry runs show the planned changes without writing to the database.
help.conditionalModeText: Advanced makes one field required. Locked makes another field read-only.
help.conditionalVisibleText: This control is only rendered while advanced controls are enabled.
help.conditionalEnabledText: This control is editable only when the audience is editors.
help.conditionalRequiredText: This control becomes required when condition mode is advanced.
help.conditionalReadOnlyText: This control becomes read-only when condition mode is locked.
help.conditionalValidatedText: This field carries a validation condition tied to the condition limit value.
help.conditionalSourceText: This select declares that its options depend on the public post type source being available.
